questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: One of the miners have been hospitalised.
options: One of the miners has been hospitalised.
One of the miners is being hospitalised.
One of the miners are hospitalised.
One of the miners were hospitalised.
questions.solution: One of the miners has been hospitalised.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The workers went on strike, ____ production.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is one of the most talented singer in the industry.
options: She is one of the most talented singer in the industry.
She is one of the talented singer in the industry.
She is one of the most talented singers in the industry.
She is the most talented singer in the industry.
questions.solution: She is one of the most talented singers in the industry.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The committee has ____ the matter thoroughly.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: It is high time we leave for the station.
options: It is high time we leave for the station.
It is high time we have left for the station.
It is high time we were leaving for the station.
It is high time we left for the station.
questions.solution: It is high time we left for the station.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The mine was ____ after the accident.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
options: She not only lost her wallet but also lost.
She lost not only her wallet but also.
She not only lost her wallet but also her phone.
She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.
questions.solution: She lost not only her wallet but also her phone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The company ____ a new subsidiary last year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is more smarter than his brother.
options: He is more smarter than his brother.
He is smart than his brother.
He is smartest than his brother.
He is smarter than his brother.
questions.solution: He is smarter than his brother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The success of the mission ____ on teamwork.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She was angry on me for being late.
options: She was angry at me for being late.
She was angry upon me for being late.
She was angry on me for being late.
She was angry with me for being late.
questions.solution: She was angry with me for being late.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The CEO ____ the concerns raised by the employees.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is suffering with high fever.
options: He is suffering from high fever.
He is suffering of high fever.
He is suffering by high fever.
He is suffering with high fever.
questions.solution: He is suffering from high fever.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The new policy is ____ to improve efficiency.

questions.stem: Correct: I have been working here from 2015.
options: I have been working here since 2015.
I have been working here from the year 2015.
I have been working here from 2015.
questions.solution: I have been working here since 2015.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government should ____ heavy penalties for violations.

questions.stem: Correct: He is more cleverer than his brother.
options: He is more clever than his brother.
He is clever than his brother.
He is more cleverer than his brother.
He is cleverer than his brother.
questions.solution: He is cleverer than his brother.

questions.stem: The government ____ the emergency protocol yesterday.

questions.stem: Rearrange: workers / demanding / are / wages / better / for
options: Workers are demanding for better wages
For better wages workers are demanding
Demanding are workers better for wages
Better wages for workers are demanding
questions.solution: Workers are demanding for better wages

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The common-emitter amplifier has:
options: High voltage gain, medium input impedance, 180° phase shift
questions.solution: CE: high gain, Z_in ≈ βr_e, output inverted. Common collector (emitter follower): gain ≈ 1.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: LDPC code: performance approaches:
questions.solution: LDPC: near-capacity performance. With iterative decoding, within 0.1 dB of Shannon limit at high SNR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 1, B = 0, C = 0. The output Y is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 250 130" width="250" height="130"><line x1="10" y1="30" x2="60" y2="30" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="55" x2="60" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="34" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">A=1</text><text x="4" y="59" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">B=0</text><path d="M60 18 L85 18 Q105 42 85 66 L60 66 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="74" y="46" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">AND</text><line x1="105" y1="42" x2="140" y2="42" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="95" x2="140" y2="95" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="4" y="99" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">C=0</text><path d="M140 30 Q150 30 165 55 Q150 80 140 80 Q150 55 140 30 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle">OR</text><line x1="165" y1="55" x2="220" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="195" y="48" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Y=?</text></svg>
figure.caption: AND–OR logic circuit

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Root locus: as gain increases from 0 to ∞, poles move from:
questions.solution: Root locus: starts at open-loop poles (K=0), ends at open-loop zeros (K=∞). Gain increases along branches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) of N points requires:
options: O(N²) operations (direct). FFT: O(N log N)
questions.solution: DFT: X[k] = Σx[n]·W_N^{kn}. FFT (Cooley-Tukey): divide and conquer, O(N log N).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SWR (Standing Wave Ratio):
questions.solution: VSWR = V_max/V_min. Ideal: 1:1. Typical: 1.5:1 or better. Impractical: > 2:1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diode forward current: I_D = I_S(e^{V_D/(nV_T)}-1). At room temp, V_D=0.7V:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Drift current is due to:
questions.solution: Drift: charged particles move under E-field. Mobility μ: velocity per unit field (cm²/Vs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interrupts in 8085:
questions.solution: TRAP = non-maskable (NMI). RST = restart (vectored to specific addresses). INTR = general (like INT).

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Dead time in inverter legs: prevents:
questions.solution: Dead time: brief interval where both switches OFF. Prevents short-circuit of DC bus through conducting switches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=220.0kΩ, Rin=4.7kΩ. Gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The wavelength λ of an electromagnetic wave of frequency f in free space is:
questions.solution: λ = c/f, with c the speed of light.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Voltage follower (buffer) has gain of:
questions.solution: Buffer: V_out = V_in. Unity gain. Infinite input impedance, zero output impedance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Poynting theorem: power delivered to volume equals:
options: Surface integral of S (Poynting vector) minus internal losses
questions.solution: Poynting: -dU/dt = div(S) + E.J. Conservation of electromagnetic energy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT bias: Vcc=9V, Rb=220kΩ, Rc=1kΩ, β=200. Base current Ib:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Brewster angle:
options: Angle where reflected wave is fully polarized (parallel component goes to zero)
questions.solution: θ_B = arctan(n₂/n₁). At Brewster, R_∥ = 0. Used in polarizing optics and antennas.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT: Vcc=10V, Rb=330kΩ, β=300. Collector current Ic:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An LED emits light due to:
questions.solution: LED: bandgap energy → photon energy (E = hν). Color depends on material (GaAs, GaP, GaN).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Steady-state error of type-0 system for ramp input:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=100.0kΩ, Rin=47.0kΩ. Gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tunnel diode operates based on:
questions.solution: Tunnel diode (Esaki): negative differential resistance due to tunneling. Fast switching.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Open-loop vs closed-loop transfer function of unity negative feedback system:
questions.solution: Closed-loop: T = G/(1+GH). Unity feedback H=1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amplifier: Rf=47kΩ, Rin=4.7kΩ. Voltage gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A field-effect transistor (FET) is a:
options: Voltage-controlled device with high input impedance
questions.solution: Gate voltage controls the channel current; the gate draws almost no current (high Zin).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gain margin is:
options: The factor by which gain can increase before instability (GM = 1/|G(jω_pc)H(jω_pc)|)
questions.solution: GM = 1/|G(jω_pc)H(jω_pc)|. ω_pc = phase crossover (∠GH = -180°). GM > 1 = stable.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=470kΩ, Rin=4.7kΩ, Vin=1V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Zener diode operates in:
questions.solution: Zener: designed to operate in reverse breakdown. V_Z is approximately constant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quarter-wave transformer matches Z_L to Z₀ using impedance:
questions.solution: λ/4: Z_in = Z_T²/Z_L. For Z_in=Z₀: Z_T = √(Z₀Z_L).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOSFET in saturation: I_D = ½·k_n'(W/L)·(V_GS-V_TH)². For k_n'(W/L)=1mA/V², V_GS-V_TH=2V:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shift register can be used as:
options: Serial-to-parallel converter, delay line, ring counter
questions.solution: Shift register: SIPO (serial in, parallel out), PISO, SISO, PIPO. Universal = 7495.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The intrinsic impedance of free space is:
questions.solution: η₀ = 376.73 Ω ≈ 120π Ω. η = √(μ/ε) for any medium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hazards — dynamic:
options: Output should change once but changes multiple times. Due to multiple paths with different delays
questions.solution: Dynamic: after the single output change. Harder to eliminate. Use synchronous design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An RC low-pass filter has R = 2 kΩ and C = 10 nF. Its cut-off frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transconductance gm of BJT at room temperature: gm = I_C/V_T. For I_C=1mA:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT bias: Vcc=15V, Rb=100kΩ, Rc=10kΩ, β=300. Base current Ib:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 12-bit ADC has a full-scale range of 10 V. Its resolution (LSB step) is about:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: QPSK vs BPSK: bandwidth efficiency:
questions.solution: QPSK: 2 bits/symbol. BPSK: 1 bit/symbol. Same bandwidth, QPSK carries double the data rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=47kΩ, R₁=2.2kΩ, Vin=5V. Vout:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: MIMO spatial multiplexing: requires:
options: Multiple antennas at TX and RX
questions.solution: Spatial multiplexing: independent data streams from each antenna. N×N MIMO can multiply capacity by N (in rich scattering).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Time-division multiplexing (TDM) shares a channel by allocating:
options: Different time slots to different signals
questions.solution: TDM interleaves samples of several signals in distinct time slots on one channel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AM modulation produces:
questions.solution: AM: s(t)=A_c[1+m(t)]cos(ω_c t) = A_c cos(ω_c t) + m(t)cos(ω_c t). Three components.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shannon's channel capacity:
questions.solution: Shannon-Hartley: fundamental limit. SNR = signal-to-noise ratio (linear, not dB).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Line codes — NRZ:
options: Non-return to zero. NRZ-L, NRZ-I. DC component, no timing (long runs)
questions.solution: NRZ: simple. DC component. No inherent clock recovery. Long 1/0 runs → timing loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: According to Shannon, channel capacity increases when:
questions.solution: C = B log₂(1 + SNR) rises with both bandwidth and signal-to-noise ratio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AM with m=1.0, input SNR=30. Output SNR (envelope detector):

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Channel capacity: B=3000kHz, SNR=10dB. Shannon limit ≈

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM has better noise immunity than AM because:
options: Noise is amplitude-based; FM uses amplitude limiters
questions.solution: FM: noise mostly AM (amplitude) → limited out. FM threshold effect below certain SNR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AM signal carries a message of highest frequency 15 kHz. Its transmission bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Error control — Hamming codes:
options: Single error correction. (7,4) code: 3 parity bits. d_min = 3
questions.solution: Hamming: (7,4), (15,11), (31,26). Parity check matrix H. Syndrome decoding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pulse width modulation (PWM):
options: Pulse width varies with modulating signal (constant amplitude)
questions.solution: PWM: duty cycle ∝ signal amplitude. Used in motor control, class D amplifiers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Link budget:
options: P_rx = P_tx + G_tx - L_tx + G_rx - L_rx - L_path - L_misc. Margins for fading
questions.solution: Link budget: include all gains (antennas, amplifiers) and losses (cables, connectors, fading margin).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A summing op-amp (Rf = 10 kΩ) has inputs 1 V via 5 kΩ and 1 V via 4 kΩ. The output is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Turbo codes:
options: Parallel concatenated convolutional codes (PCCC). Iterative decoding. Near Shannon limit
questions.solution: Turbo: two RSC encoders + interleaver. Iterative (MAP/Log-MAP). 3G/4G. LTE turbo.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=220kΩ, R₁=2.2kΩ, Vin=0.5V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM vs AM noise immunity:
options: FM: superior SNR. Pre-emphasis/de-emphasis improves high-f SNR
questions.solution: FM: threshold effect. FM SNR improvement: (3/2)·β² for β>1. Capture effect.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Class B amplifier:
options: Conduction angle 180°. Push-pull. Efficiency up to 78.5%. Crossover distortion
questions.solution: Class B: each transistor conducts half cycle. Crossover distortion at V_BE threshold.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AM power:
options: P_total = P_c(1 + m²/2). Sideband power = m²·P_c/2. Efficiency = m²/(2+m²)
questions.solution: Efficiency max: 33.3% at m=1 (DSB-FC). Only 1/6 total power in sidebands.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Switching regulators — buck-boost:
options: V_out = -V_in·D/(1-D). Inverted output. Can step up or down
questions.solution: Buck-boost: inverting topology. SEPIC: non-inverting buck-boost. Cuk: low ripple.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: FM signal bandwidth (Carson's rule):
questions.solution: B ≈ 2(Δf + fm). Δf = peak frequency deviation, fm = max modulating frequency. For Δf >> fm, B ≈ 2Δf.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Schmitt trigger hysteresis voltage: V_hyst = V_high - V_low. For R₁=10k, R₂=20k, Vcc=5V:
questions.solution: V_UT = Vcc×R₁/(R₁+R₂) = 5×10/30 ≈ 1.67V. Hysteresis width = 2×V_UT for symmetric thresholds.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Q-point of a transistor amplifier:
options: DC operating point (sets collector current and V_CE)
questions.solution: Q-point: DC bias. Thermal runaway: Q-point shifts with temperature. Fixed by negative feedback.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Op-amp — differentiator:
options: Vout = -RC·dVin/dt. High-pass behavior. Stability issues at high f
questions.solution: Differentiator: capacitor at input. Noise amplification at high frequencies. Series R limits gain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A voltage follower (buffer) has gain:
questions.solution: Voltage follower: V_out = V_in. High Z_in, low Z_out.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Feedback effect on input impedance:
questions.solution: Series feedback: adds voltage. Shunt feedback: adds current to input node.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMA (Direct Memory Access):
options: Peripheral ↔ memory without CPU. DMA controller. Cycle stealing or burst mode
questions.solution: DMA: 1+ channel. Transfer types: single, block, demand. Scatter-gather for physical memory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A varactor diode is used as:
questions.solution: Varactor: junction capacitance varies with reverse voltage. Used in VCO, tuners, phase shifters.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crossover distortion occurs in:
options: Class B amplifiers (transition between positive and negative halves)
questions.solution: Crossover: dead zone near zero crossing. Fixed by Class AB (slight bias).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Nyquist sampling theorem: minimum sampling rate for signal bandwidth B Hz:
questions.solution: Nyquist rate = 2×bandwidth. Aliasing occurs if f_s < 2B.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Single-phase full bridge inverter output frequency controlled by:
questions.solution: Inverter frequency = switching frequency. Square wave or PWM. PWM allows harmonic control.

questions.stem: A rectifier circuit is used to convert:
questions.solution: Rectifiers (using diodes) convert alternating voltage into unidirectional (DC) voltage.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Snubber circuit purpose in power electronics:
questions.solution: Snubber: RC or RCD circuit across switch. Limits dv/dt and di/dt. Protects from switching transients.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Class B amplifier efficiency maximum is:
questions.solution: Class B: η_max = π/4 ≈ 78.5%. Class A: 25%. Class AB: between A and B.
